key_insight: FEVessel extends pretrained finite-element surrogates to 3D pressure vessels by learning from physics-based energy instead of FEM labels, enabling mesh-independent prediction and solver warm-starting.
engineering_relevance: It can reduce analysis cost and manual mesh repair for industrial pressure-vessel simulation while remaining robust across materials, geometries, loads, and degraded meshes.
method: Encode each vessel as a point cloud with coordinate, material, and load channels.
Pretrain a Transolver on total potential energy rather than labeled FEM displacements/stresses.
Use the prediction to warm-start iterative solvers such as AMG.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 1.35% displacement error, 2.07% strain error, 18 AMG iterations vs 195, a 9.2× wall-clock speedup, and <3.66% error on inverted/sliver meshes.
watch_item: Performance is reported for a specific pressure-vessel setting, so generalization to other solid mechanics problems and production-grade integration should be validated.
paper_title: FEVessel: Mesh-Independent Analysis of 3D Pressure Vessels with the Label-Free Pretrained Finite Element Method
